title: Upgrade fleet to the Nate-native Ringer + Paperclip pattern
purpose: Make Ringer a frontier-orchestrated, executable-evaluation, economical-worker control loop whose immutable receipts improve later routing. Paperclip records triggers, progress, and work products without becoming lifecycle authority.
requirements: Add optional run-level risk, orchestrator, and contract_review metadata to Ringer manifests without breaking existing manifests.
Compute a deterministic SHA-256 contract hash from the semantic worker contract: run name plus each task key, spec, check, expected files, engine, model, task type, and verification sentence.
Expose the risk, orchestrator metadata, contract-review metadata, and contract hash in live/final run state and attempt evidence.

For high or critical risk, lint must report a finding unless a cross-family reviewer is harness-attested and has a PASS verdict. A semantic reviewer can veto green checks but can never turn a red executable check into PASS.
Ship a reusable Nate-native template and fleet policy describing canonical, contract-reviewed, semantic-review, and bakeoff modes.
Keep the one-retry rule and inject raw failure output into that retry. Do not add indefinite retries.
Use existing task_type/model scoreboard data for worker selection; do not encode a permanent global model ranking.
Keep Ringside observational and Paperclip non-authoritative. No new queue, dispatch gate, or lifecycle ledger is allowed.
Preserve backwards compatibility for existing tests, manifests, hooks, receipts, and dashboard consumers.

worker.selection_evidence: Ringer scoreboard: code-feature 23 tasks, 23 first-try passes

implementation_lanes.deliverable: A binary-applicable patch exported outside the worker worktree.

worker.selection_evidence: Ringer scoreboard: code-feature 23 tasks, 23 first-try passes

implementation_lanes.deliverable: A binary-applicable patch exported outside the worker worktree.

worker_boundaries: Each worker receives this complete contract plus only its owned paths and must leave changes uncommitted.
Workers run in isolated Git worktrees and may not push, merge, restart services, edit Paperclip state, or touch the hot Aegis checkout.
The orchestrator integrates exported patches only after the task-local executable check passes.
A worker cannot declare completion; Ringer's executed check decides PASS or FAIL.
A failed task receives exactly one retry with Ringer's raw verifier output.
acceptance: The frozen contract receives a fresh, harness-attested Claude Opus 4.8 PASS before worker dispatch.
Ringer lint accepts the concrete upgrade manifest and reports no finding that undermines executable verification or worker isolation.
Both gpt-5.4-mini worker tasks PASS under Ringer; any failure is retried at most once with raw verifier output.
The two exported patches apply cleanly to the isolated integration branch.
Targeted tests for contract metadata and the template pass.
The complete Ringer test suite passes from the integrated branch.
A fresh cross-family semantic review of the integrated diff returns PASS; it may not override a red test.
A real post-integration Ringer canary linked to JAC-3643 and notes-hbq1q emits a finished state containing the contract hash and attested identities.
Paperclip comments/work products and Ringside API readback point to the same immutable run/receipt and do not claim lifecycle authority.
The implementation commit, artifact hashes, verification commands, and rollback command are captured in a durable handoff.
falsifiers: Existing manifests fail to parse or existing tests regress.
High/critical manifests can silently omit an independent PASS contract review.
The contract hash changes when JSON key order changes but semantic task fields do not.
Paperclip or Ringside becomes an authoritative lifecycle store or blocks dispatch.
A worker writes outside its owned paths or the hot checkout is mutated before integration.
A semantic review turns a failed executable check into PASS.
A retry occurs more than once or omits raw failure context.

integration.deploy_mode: content-addressed additive deployment after commit and full verification
integration.rollback: Revert the isolated implementation commit and restore the prior live Ringer file set; do not reset or clean the dirty Aegis checkout.

out_of_scope: Cold restart of Paperclip, Ringside, Aegis, or Talaris.
Implementing the separate Obsidian CLI projection design.
Making Analyst-Opus, Sol, Luna, or any worker model a permanent global default.
Replacing executable checks with model opinion.
